summary: This is a brief status update post where Joel asks readers for recommendations on GUI automated testing tools for CityDesk, their Windows desktop product. He wants to build an automated test suite to address regression bugs during the beta period. He also notes being buried in deferred work now that the beta has shipped.
key_points: Regression bugs were a real problem: they were breaking two things for every one they fixed, motivating investment in automated testing
Joel wanted affordable testing tools, explicitly ruling out expensive enterprise solutions that require sales demos
Shipping a beta creates a backlog of deferred work that hits all at once afterward
money_quotes: I'm going to take advantage of the CityDesk beta to develop an automated test suite (so we don't keep breaking 2 things for every 1 thing we fix!)
It needs to support Windows apps and be the sort of thing that doesn't involve a salesperson visiting your office with a $6000 projector for the demo (in other words, not too expensive.)
Now I'm snowed under with all the stuff I was deferring until the beta shipped
key_insight: Automated testing becomes urgent when regression bugs outpace fixes, but small companies need affordable tools without enterprise sales overhead.
